description: Native American reservation in Dakota, United States
city in Oklahoma, United States and county seat of Tulsa County
description_wiki: Tulsa (  TUL-sə) is the second-most-populous city in the U.S. state of Oklahoma and the 48th-most populous city in the United States.

description: country in North America
description_wiki: Mexico, officially the United Mexican States, is a country in North America.

description: emperor of Mexico (1832-1867)
description_wiki: Maximilian I (Spanish: Fernando Maximiliano José María de Habsburgo-Lorena; German: Ferdinand Maximilian Josef Maria von Habsburg-Lothringen; 6 July 1832 – 19 June 1867) was an Austrian archduke who became emperor of the Second Mexican Empire from 10 April 1864 until his execution by the Mexican Republic on 19 June 1867.

description: human settlement in United States of America
description_wiki: Pierce is an unincorporated community in McIntosh County, Oklahoma, United States.

description: nation of American Indians
ancient Tiwa pueblo in Taos County, New Mexico, United States
description_wiki: Taos Pueblo (or Pueblo de Taos) is an ancient pueblo belonging to a Taos-speaking (Tiwa) Native American tribe of Puebloan people.

description: town in and county sear of Taos County, New Mexico, United States
description_wiki: Taos () is a town in Taos County, in the north-central region of New Mexico.

description: Potawatomi artist from Oklahoma (1912-1989)
description_wiki: Woodrow Wilson Crumbo (1912—1989) was a Native American artist and educator from Oklahoma.

description: city in Cleveland County, Oklahoma, United States
description_wiki: Lexington is a city in Cleveland County, Oklahoma, United States.

description: president of the United States from 1913 to 1921 (1856–1924)
description_wiki: Thomas Woodrow Wilson (December 28, 1856 – February 3, 1924) was the 28th president of the United States, serving from 1913 to 1921.

description: American singer-songwriter (1912–1967)
description_wiki: Woodrow Wilson Guthrie (; July 14, 1912 – October 3, 1967) was an American singer, songwriter,  and composer widely considered one of the most significant figures in American folk music.

description: Native American people of the Great Plains
description_wiki: The Potawatomi ( ), also spelled Pottawatomi and Pottawatomie (among many variations), are an Indigenous North American tribe of the Great Plains, upper Mississippi River, and western Great Lakes region.

description: city in Osage and Tulsa counties, Oklahoma, United States
description_wiki: Sand Springs is a city in Osage, Creek and Tulsa counties in the U.S. state of Oklahoma.

description: city in Tulsa and Creek counties in Oklahoma, United States, that is the county seat of Creek County
description_wiki: Sapulpa is a city in and the county seat of Creek County, extending partly into Tulsa County, in the U.S. state of Oklahoma.

description: capital and most populous city of the Netherlands
description_wiki: Amsterdam (Dutch: [ˌɑmstərˈdɑm] ; lit. 'Dam in the Amstel') is the capital and largest city of the Netherlands.

description: 1949–1990 country in central Europe, unified into modern Germany
description_wiki: East Germany, officially the German Democratic Republic (GDR), was a country in Central Europe from its formation on 7 October 1949 until its reunification with West Germany (Federal Republic of Germany) on 3 October 1990.

aliases: Wally Fowler and The Georgia Clodhoppers
description: American country and gospel vocal quartet
town in Harz District, Saxony-Anhalt, Germany
description_wiki: Halberstadt (German pronunciation: [ˈhalbɐˌʃtat] ; Eastphalian: Halverstidde) is a town in the state of Saxony-Anhalt in central Germany, the capital of Harz district.

description: historic school near Newkirk, Oklahoma, United States
description_wiki: Chilocco Indian School (/ʃɪˈlɑkoʊ/) was an agricultural school for Native Americans on reserved land in north-central Oklahoma from 1884 to 1980.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Kansas (  KAN-zəss) is a landlocked state in the Midwestern region of the United States.

description: nonprofit organization in Bozeman, United States
city in and county seat of Sedgwick County, Kansas, United States
description_wiki: Wichita ( , WITCH-ih-taw) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Kansas and the county seat of Sedgwick County.

description: founder of the Kiowa Indian School of Art
description_wiki: Susie Peters (Kiowa name: Kom-tah-gya) was an American preservationist and matron at the Anadarko Agency, who worked to promote Kiowa artists.

description: public university in Wichita, Kansas, U.S.
description_wiki: Wichita State University (WSU) is a public research university in Wichita, Kansas, United States.

description: United States government New Deal agency (1935–1939) employing millions of unemployed people to carry out public works projects
description_wiki: The Works Progress Administration (WPA; from 1935 to 1939, then known as the Work Projects Administration from 1939 to 1943) was an American New Deal agency that employed millions of jobseekers (mostly men who were not formally educated) to carry out public works projects, including the construction of public buildings and roads.

description: president of the United States from 1933 to 1945 (1882–1945)
description_wiki: Franklin Delano Roosevelt (January 30, 1882 – April 12, 1945), also known as FDR, was the 32nd president of the United States, serving from 1933 until his death in 1945.
